cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which statement is true according to the passage. The options are A, B, C, D. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I get the details right. The passage starts by talking about memory and how it can be improved with practice. Then it gives examples of memory "tricks." The first example is about the writer finding the front door unlocked one morning, yet clearly remembering locking it the night before. So here, the writer thought they locked the door (they remember doing it), but in reality, it was left unlocked. That's a case of memory tricking them into thinking they did something when they didn't. Looking at the options: A) One night the writer forgot to lock the front door. Well, according to the passage, the writer found the door unlocked in the morning and remembered locking it the night before. So the actual fact is that they didn't lock it, but they thought they did. So A says they forgot to lock it, which is true in reality, but the question is about what the passage states. The passage says that the writer thought they locked it, but it was actually left unlocked. So A would be correct in reality, but the passage is about the memory trick. Let me check the options again. B) One night the writer forgot having locked the front door. Hmm. No, the passage says the writer remembered locking it. They didn't forget having locked it; they remembered it, but it wasn't true. So B is not correct. The writer didn't forget having locked it—they thought they did lock it, but in reality, they didn't. So their memory tricked them into thinking they did lock it, but they actually didn't. So the correct answer should relate to the writer thinking they locked it when they didn't. Looking at the options again. The question is which is true according to the passage. The passage says: "One morning last week, for example, I got up and found that I had left the front door unlocked all night, yet I clearly remember locking it carefully the night before." So the writer left the door unlocked (they didn't lock it), but remembered locking it. So the correct answer would be A or B? Let me check the options again. A) The writer forgot to lock the door (i.e., they didn't lock it, which is what actually happened). But the passage says the writer thought they did lock it. So the writer forgot to lock it (A) is what actually happened. But the passage is about the memory trick. The passage says that the writer's memory told them they locked it when they didn't. So the fact is that they forgot to lock it. So A is true according to the passage. But let me check the other options. B) The writer forgot having locked the door. No, the writer didn't forget having locked it; they remembered it. The writer's memory trick is that they remembered locking it when they didn't. So B is not correct because it's saying they forgot having locked it, which would be if they did lock it but now can't remember.
